text:
Market today: Mixed mood with no clean catalyst for tech. The real driver today is headline #8 - JPMorgan flagging that retail traders pulling back on options and margin is a headwind specifically for high-beta tech. That's your world.

Your holdings: Three need attention. PLTR (-12.77%) has a SELL flag - you're deep in the red on margin, stop at $85 still far away but the signal says act. AVGO (-12.45%) is your largest position with a TRIM flag - being your biggest weight while down double digits on margin is a compounding risk. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history - tiny buffer to your $150 stop, treat it as a hand grenade, not a core hold. Margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: 1) Whether retail options/margin data confirms further pullback - that directly pressures your chip and tech names. 2) AVGO price action - as your largest position, it sets the tone for your whole book.

Bottom line: Your biggest fires are PLTR and AVGO - decide your trim/exit plan before the market decides for you.
